jokes.text: Charlie wanting to get a good seat at a sexual harassment seminar
jokes.setup: Sexual harassment seminar is serious business
jokes.punchline: Charlie treats it like entertainment and wants front row seats
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - treating inappropriate situations with childlike enthusiasm. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: Frank: 'Back in the day, I banged all my secretaries. That's the way you did it. You hire some girl with no experience, you bang her, you promote her. It's a win-win situation for everybody, except for the wives.'
jokes.punchline: Frank casually describes textbook harassment as the good old days with business logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - completely missing the point while being accidentally honest about terrible behavior. The 'except for the wives' tag is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: 'Back in the day, I banged all my secretaries. That's the way you did it. You hire some girl with no experience, you bang her, you promote her. It's a win-win situation for everybody, except for the wives.'

jokes.text: 'You DENNIS'd someone probably' - using Dennis's name as a verb for sexual harassment
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out why they're on the harassment list
jokes.punchline: Dennis's behavior is so notorious they turned his name into a verb
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of character history - turning a character's name into a verb for their signature awful behavior is comedy gold.

dialogue.line: You DENNIS'd someone probably

jokes.text: The only reason we're here is because you DENNIS'd someone probably and she put us on the list.
jokes.punchline: Using Dennis's name as a verb for sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific joke. 'DENNIS'd' as a verb is highly quotable and shows the characters' awareness of Dennis's patterns.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The only reason we're here is because you DENNIS'd someone probably and she put us on the list.

jokes.text: Dennis's paranoid rant about women being 'on a rampage' ending with 'Who could possibly be happy right now?' followed immediately by the instructor's cheerful 'Hey-o! Who's ready to party?!'
jokes.setup: Dennis's doom and gloom about the current climate
jokes.punchline: Instructor's oblivious enthusiasm creates perfect tonal whiplash
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition comedy - the timing and tonal shift work perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Who could possibly be happy right now?

dialogue.speaker: Instructor
dialogue.line: Hey-o! Who's ready to party?!

jokes.text: Charlie asking 'how many points do we need to score' and insisting everything is graded by points because 'how could the Eagles have won the Super Bowl?'
jokes.punchline: Charlie applies sports logic to harassment training with unshakeable confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - applying completely wrong framework with total conviction. Philadelphia Eagles reference adds local flavor.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: How many points do we need to score?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Everything is graded by points because how could the Eagles have won the Super Bowl?

jokes.text: Everything is graded by points, right? I mean, otherwise, how could the Eagles have won the Super Bowl, right?
jokes.punchline: Using Eagles Super Bowl win as evidence for point-based reality
jokes.explanation: Charlie logic in action. Philadelphia-specific reference adds local flavor while showing his warped reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Everything is graded by points, right? I mean, otherwise, how could the Eagles have won the Super Bowl, right?

jokes.text: Frank asking about statute of limitations, then immediately leaving when he learns some states might eliminate it, muttering 'They got nothing on me past '92, tops'
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately realizes he's in danger and bolts with specific year cutoff
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - casual admission of decades of wrongdoing through his panic. The specific '92 tops' detail is golden.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: They got nothing on me past '92, tops

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Nevada

jokes.text: The guys immediately perking up when male instructor Alan arrives: 'Oh! Here comes the boss man. Finally, a dude. Yeah, okay, cool. He's in charge.'
jokes.punchline: Gang immediately assumes male assistant is the real authority
jokes.explanation: Solid commentary on ingrained sexism, though somewhat predictable for these characters.

dialogue.line: Oh! Here comes the boss man. Finally, a dude. Yeah, okay, cool. He's in charge.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie

dialogue.line: 'Go Birds' becoming a group rallying cry in the middle of harassment seminar

jokes.text: Role-play actor being criticized for mumbling his lines and not being believable rather than the harassment content
jokes.setup: What did the boss do wrong in harassment scenario?

jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - they miss the point entirely and focus on theatrical criticism.

dialogue.line: Role-play actor being criticized for mumbling his lines and not being believable rather than the harassment content

jokes.text: Yeah. He mumbled his lines. I didn't believe a word out of his mouth.
jokes.setup: Asked what boss did wrong in harassment scenario
jokes.punchline: Charlie critiquing acting performance instead of harassment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Charlie completely missing the point while giving theater criticism is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah. He mumbled his lines. I didn't believe a word out of his mouth.

jokes.text: 'But it's not for her. It's about her' and 'we haven't seen the girl's boobs. Can we see them?' completely missing the point of harassment
jokes.setup: Explanation of why harassment is wrong
jokes.punchline: Characters demonstrate the exact problem by requesting to see the referenced body parts
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of how they embody the exact problem being taught. Self-defeating behavior.

dialogue.line: But it's not for her. It's about her
we haven't seen the girl's boobs. Can we see them?

doorman
gay guy

He's our doorman.

jokes.explanation: Charlie's method acting approach to simple role-play is wonderfully overthought and unnecessary.

jokes.text: Mac physically throwing Dee/customer before she even speaks, followed by 'That was amazing! You got me, like, a foot off the ground!'
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately commits assault, Dee is excited about the physical achievement

dialogue.speaker: Dee

jokes.text: And how many drinks have I had at this point? I've had five.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: And how many drinks have I had at this point? I've had five.

dialogue.speaker: Dee

jokes.text: Dee asking if constantly calling a female coworker a bird is harassment, followed by 'Nice try, you dumb bitch'

jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical moment - Dennis's narcissistic worldview reduces harassment to attractiveness. Perfectly wrong and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Dennis's conclusion: 'We should probably stop harassing them.' 'Uh, well, you know, I don't know. One step at a time. We don't want the whole system to collapse.'
jokes.setup: Seeming moment of growth and understanding
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately backpedals and wants to maintain harassment gradually
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - moment of seeming enlightenment immediately undermined by concern for 'the system.' Classic Dennis.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We should probably stop harassing them.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Uh, well, you know, I don't know. One step at a time. We don't want the whole system to collapse.

jokes.text: Agreeing they should 'probably' stop harassing women but 'one step at a time'
jokes.setup: Discussing the lessons learned from the seminar
jokes.punchline: Treating stopping harassment as a gradual lifestyle change rather than basic decency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating basic human decency as an inconvenient lifestyle change.

dialogue.line: Agreeing they should 'probably' stop harassing women but 'one step at a time'

jokes.text: Dee's 'MeToo' pun when they mention going for drinks, followed by 'Very good, and for a woman, a good joke.'
jokes.punchline: Dee makes MeToo/me too pun, Dennis gives backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with classic Dennis condescension. The 'for a woman' qualifier is perfectly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: MeToo

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Very good, and for a woman, a good joke.

jokes.text: Mac asking about Alan's dick size and claiming he 'sought it out' in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Mac admits to actively looking at another man's penis
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac behavior - inappropriate sexual interest disguised as casual observation. 'Sought it out' makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
